title: World War II - European Theater (1944-1945)
gmStyle: Second person, present tense. Hedgerows, ruined villages, K-rations, and gallows humor between shellings. The squad is family. NPCs have hometowns, nicknames, and tells. Scenes are 2-4 paragraphs and end on a hook, a choice, or a question.

ratingOverlays.story: Danger is real but violence reads like a classic adventure serial: tracer light, close calls, and consequences without gore. Deaths happen off-page and are honored. The enemy is fought, not cartooned. Themes of brotherhood, courage, and getting each other home carry every scene.
ratingOverlays.heroic: Combat has genuine stakes and loss, described with the intensity of a PG-13 war film. Wounds and fear are acknowledged without lingering. Liberated towns and grateful families show what the fight is for.
ratingOverlays.historical: Honest to the era - the meat-grinder of the bocage, combat fatigue, and the cost of liberation are not softened, but nothing is gratuitous. The tone honors the people who lived it.
